todaysEvents.summary: Trump (Apr 2) vows 'extremely hard' strikes for 2-3 weeks - 'bring them back to the Stone Ages' (Axios, NBC/CNBC Apr 2); full reversal of Apr 1 'leaving' signal; Brent surges +8.3% to $109.03 (Gulf News/CNBC Apr 3).

Iran launches massive multi-vector attack on UAE (Apr 2): 457 ballistic missiles + 2,038 drones intercepted; 11 dead, 191 injured; also strikes Israel (4 separate barrages in 6 hrs), Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Kuwait simultaneously; largest single-day attack of the conflict.

Philippines strikes bilateral deal with Iran: toll-free Hormuz passage for Philippine vessels and safe return of Filipino seafarers (Bloomberg, The National Apr 2); first ASEAN nation to secure bilateral deal. April 6 deadline 3 days away; Iran denies negotiations; 'trust level is at zero' (Iranian FM).

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Trump's escalation rhetoric proves negotiating posture: a back-channel deal materialises before April 6 - Iran accepts partial nuclear rollback, toll booth suspended, US refrains from Kharg seizure. Bilateral deals (India, Philippines) provide the template for a broader framework. Brent retreats to $90-100/bbl; JKM pulls back to $16-18/MMBtu on Qatar damage assessment resuming. P&I cover only partially restored - toll booth precedent means full commercial Hormuz passage requires a separate legal framework.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: April 6 deadline passes with limited US action: Iran endures strikes on power grid and missile infrastructure but maintains toll booth and bilateral deal framework. US-Israel avoid Kharg seizure; ceasefire talks drag into May. UAE, Saudi Arabia absorb continuing strikes but do not cut oil output materially. IRGC cyberattacks on Western tech firms cause selective disruptions. Brent settles $105-130/bbl; JKM at $19-24/MMBtu. iTraxx Asia IG holds 185-200bp.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: April 6 triggers Kharg Island seizure or resumed power plant strikes; Iran responds by closing the toll corridor entirely and launching ground-war-scale attacks on Saudi Aramco infrastructure. UAE attacks escalate beyond interception capacity. IRGC executes cyberattacks on US tech firms disrupting Apple and Nvidia supply chains. Houthis escalate to daily strikes on Israel; ground invasion preparations confirmed. Ras Laffan offline for the quarter; Saudi output cuts 3-5M bpd. Oil hits $160-185/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk critical.
